behavior_summary: Dual-clock FIFO with 17 storage entries of 8 bits each. Writes are initiated from the push domain by asserting push_valid for one push_clk cycle; the corresponding push_data item is accepted into FIFO storage subject to available capacity/credits. Reads are completed in the pop domain on a pop_clk edge when pop_valid and pop_ready are both high; that handshake dequeues exactly one item. When data is available but not consumed, pop_valid remains asserted and pop_data remains stable until consumption. pop_valid and pop_data are combinationally derived rather than fully registered, allowing an empty FIFO to expose newly pushed data through a cut-through path. The push side uses credit return flow control: each successfully dequeued item eventually causes a one-cycle push_credit pulse in the push clock domain unless credit return is stalled by push_credit_stall. The write-side reset condition is the logical OR of push_rst and push_sender_in_reset, with reset effect delayed by one push_clk cycle due to internal reset registration. The read side is reset by pop_rst, also with a one-pop_clk-cycle delayed effect.
edge_cases: FIFO nominal depth is 17 entries, so occupancy and slot counters must represent values from 0 through 17.
push_full should indicate no free space; writes attempted while full are not explicitly defined as accepted, so a strict testbench should assume accepted writes require available space.
pop_empty should indicate no stored data; reads only dequeue on pop_valid && pop_ready.
When pop_valid is high and pop_ready is low, pop_data must remain stable and the item must not be removed.

Because pop_valid and pop_data are unregistered, an empty FIFO may show cut-through behavior from a newly pushed item to the pop side; exact same-cycle visibility across asynchronous domains is not safely cycle-checkable.
Each successful pop returns one credit pulse to the push domain, but the exact push_clk cycle of the returned credit is affected by CDC synchronization and is not fully specified.
If push_credit_stall is asserted, return of credits can be paused; delayed credits should not be assumed lost unless the spec explicitly says so, which it does not.
The write-side logic remains in reset while either push_rst or push_sender_in_reset is asserted, due to OR-combined reset intent.
Both push_rst and pop_rst are synchronous and internally registered, so visible reset effects are delayed by one cycle in their respective domains rather than immediate.
credit_available_push is described as credit_count_push minus credit_withhold_push; behavior for underflow if withhold exceeds count is not explicitly specified.

push_slots, credit_count_push, credit_available_push, and pop_items are status quantities crossing or derived from cross-domain state; exact phase relationship to true occupancy may lag because of synchronizers.
Simultaneous push and pop activity can occur on independent clocks; FIFO ordering must remain first-in-first-out for accepted transactions even when operations overlap in time.
state_transitions: Push-domain reset state -> active push-domain operation after the internally registered write-side reset deasserts.
Pop-domain reset state -> active pop-domain operation after the internally registered read-side reset deasserts.
Logical FIFO occupancy N -> N+1 when an item is accepted on the push side.
Logical FIFO occupancy N -> N-1 when an item is consumed on the pop side.
Empty state (occupancy 0) -> non-empty state when the first item is accepted.
Non-empty state -> empty state when the last available item is consumed.
Not-full state -> full state when occupancy reaches 17.
Full state -> not-full state when at least one item is consumed.
Held-output pop state -> same held-output state while pop_valid=1 and pop_ready=0, with identical pop_data until the eventual consuming handshake.

Credit-return pending conceptually arises after each successful pop and remains pending until a push_credit pulse is emitted in the push domain, unless stalled; exact microstates are implementation-specific and not fully specified.
priorities: On the push side, reset condition (push_rst or push_sender_in_reset) overrides normal write-side operation.
On the pop side, pop_rst reset behavior overrides normal read-side operation.
For dequeuing, pop_ready only has effect when pop_valid is high; the handshake condition is pop_valid && pop_ready.
For output holding, retaining existing pop_data/pop_valid under backpressure has priority over advancing to the next item.
For returned credits, push_credit_stall suppresses or pauses credit pulse emission relative to normal credit return behavior.
If the FIFO is full, full-condition handling must prevent further accepted writes before space is freed, though exact signaling/ignore semantics for illegal push_valid are not fully specified.
mutation_risks: Incorrect FIFO depth handling, especially treating the buffer as 16 entries instead of 17.
Off-by-one errors in push_full, pop_empty, push_slots, or pop_items around transitions 0<->1 and 16<->17.
Wrong reset semantics, such as asynchronous instead of synchronous behavior, missing the one-cycle delayed reset effect, or ignoring push_sender_in_reset.
Failing to OR push_sender_in_reset with push_rst on the write side.
Incorrect handling of pop backpressure, such as changing pop_data while pop_ready is low.
Dropping cut-through capability or incorrectly registering pop_valid/pop_data, causing extra latency when FIFO is empty.
Improper combinational cut-through that violates hold behavior during pop_valid && !pop_ready.
Returning push_credit on push-side writes instead of on successful pops.
Producing push_credit for every pop but ignoring push_credit_stall.
Losing, duplicating, or permanently suppressing credits after a temporary push_credit_stall.
Mishandling FIFO ordering under overlapping push/pop activity across the two clocks.
Incorrect synchronizer-induced status behavior, such as directly using unsynchronized pointers for full/empty logic.
Incorrect arithmetic or width handling for credit_available_push = credit_count_push - credit_withhold_push.
Updating registered/status outputs in the wrong clock domain or one cycle late/early.
executable_reference: Model accepted pushes as events on push_clk where write-side reset is inactive and the FIFO is allowed to accept data; enqueue push_data values in order.
Model accepted pops only on pop_clk edges where pop_valid && pop_ready; each accepted pop must match the oldest not-yet-popped enqueued value.
If pop_valid is high and pop_ready is low across consecutive pop_clk cycles, pop_data must remain unchanged and no item may be removed from the reference queue.
No output item may appear more times than it was accepted on the push side, and accepted items may not disappear without a matching pop handshake.
Ordering must be strict FIFO: the sequence of completed pop handshakes equals the sequence of accepted push_data transactions.

While write-side reset is active, treat the push side as reset-blocked and require push_receiver_in_reset to indicate reset state; exact acceptance during the one-cycle internal reset pipeline should follow the delayed-reset rule rather than immediate reset.
While read-side reset is active, do not require normal pop-side data delivery until the internally registered reset effect has cleared.

When a pop handshake completes, eventually exactly one push_credit pulse should be returned in the push clock domain unless stalled; during push_credit_stall, absence of pulse is allowed, but pulses must not exceed the number of completed pops.
push_credit pulses are one push_clk cycle wide when emitted.
When the FIFO is observably empty, pop_empty should assert; when a valid item is being persistently offered and not yet consumed, pop_empty should not simultaneously indicate nonexistence of data.
push_full should block further accepted writes once effective capacity is exhausted, with effective capacity potentially influenced by credit configuration.
guaranteed_properties: The module is a dual-clock FIFO connecting a push_clk producer to a pop_clk consumer.
Data width is 8 bits and storage capacity is specified as 17 entries.
Writes are initiated on push_clk by a one-cycle push_valid assertion carrying push_data.
Reads complete on pop_clk only when both pop_valid and pop_ready are high.
If pop_valid is high and pop_ready is low, the FIFO holds the same data on pop_data until the handshake completes.
pop_valid and pop_data are not internally registered, enabling a combinational cut-through path when the FIFO is empty.
push_rst and pop_rst are active-high synchronous resets.
The effect of push_rst and pop_rst is delayed by one clock cycle in their respective domains because the resets are internally registered.
The write-side reset condition remains asserted as long as either push_rst or push_sender_in_reset is asserted.
push_receiver_in_reset indicates the FIFO reset state to the upstream module.
A successful pop causes a returned credit represented by a one-cycle push_credit pulse in the push clock domain.
push_credit can be paused by push_credit_stall.
The design uses 2-stage synchronizers for cross-domain pointer/status communication.
ambiguous_properties: Whether push_valid alone always implies write acceptance, or only when the sender has credits / FIFO is not full; the spec describes initiation but does not define illegal-overflow behavior precisely.
The exact latency, phase alignment, and pulse timing from a successful pop to the corresponding push_credit pulse in the push_clk domain.

Whether delayed credits during push_credit_stall are queued and eventually replayed, or whether some alternative accounting is used internally, though functional loss of credits would be questionable.
The exact arithmetic semantics of credit_available_push when credit_withhold_push exceeds credit_count_push, including whether the result saturates, wraps, or is constrained by legal configuration.
The exact reset values and update timing of push_slots, credit_count_push, credit_available_push, and pop_items immediately after reset and during CDC settling.
The exact same-cycle truth table for pop_valid/pop_data during cut-through when push and pop side events occur near each other across asynchronous clocks.
Whether pop_empty and pop_valid are always exact logical complements under all cut-through and boundary cases.
The exact behavior of push_full, push_slots, and pop_items during simultaneous cross-domain push/pop activity when synchronizer lag exists.
Whether push_receiver_in_reset is combinational from reset intent or itself delayed/registered in the push domain.
The exact accepted-write capacity under credit_withhold_push and credit_initial_push configurations if those values do not correspond cleanly to the physical depth of 17.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume a successful pop produces push_credit in a fixed number of push_clk cycles.
Do not assume push_credit is synchronous to pop_clk or directly observable in the same cycle as the pop.
Do not assume pop_valid/pop_data cut-through has a deterministic same-cycle timing relationship across asynchronous clocks beyond the functional possibility of cut-through.
Do not assume push_slots equals exactly 17 minus true occupancy on every push_clk cycle without CDC lag.
Do not assume pop_items equals exactly true occupancy on every pop_clk cycle without CDC lag.
Do not assume credit_count_push or credit_available_push update immediately on every remote-domain event.
Do not assume credit_available_push is nonnegative unless the configuration guarantees credit_withhold_push <= credit_count_push.
Do not assume pop_empty is always exactly the inverse of pop_valid in all intermediate cut-through situations unless explicitly proven by RTL.
Do not assume writes presented while full are accepted, ignored, or overwrite old data unless the test is explicitly checking safe rejection behavior.
Do not assume read-side outputs are cleared immediately on reset assertion; reset effect is specified as delayed by one domain clock.
Do not assume push_receiver_in_reset deasserts immediately when push_rst and push_sender_in_reset deassert; one-cycle registration may apply.
Do not assume an exact cycle-accurate internal pointer or synchronizer implementation beyond externally visible FIFO ordering and handshake semantics.
